LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER SHOULD NOT BE CONFIRMED - PARTIES REPRESENT TO THE COURT THAT THEY HAVE REACHED A STIPULATION REGARDING THIS MATTER. THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER SHOULD NOT BE CONFIRMED, AND SIGNS PARTIES' STIPULATED ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 425.16
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. PHIL FOSTER, ESQ. of Tour-Sarkissian Law Offices, LLP, Tel No. 415-626-7744 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. MATTHEW J. GLUCK, ESQ. of Gluck Daniel LLP, Tel No. 415-510-2114 appearing for Defendant Diane Knight.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT DIANE KNIGHT'S Special Motion To Strike Complaint Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 425.16, Ms. Knight's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is denied. Defendant satisfied the first prong of CCP 425.16 because the first cause of action is based in part on the non-incidental allegation that Ms. Knight breached her fiduciary duty by threatening to sue Mr. Adnan Mehmood. (Malin v. Singer (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1283 (attorney's demand letter in anticipation of litigation was petitioning activity that implicated the anti-SLAPP statute; Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. v. Happening House Ventures (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539 (defendant met first prong of anti-SLAPP statute where only 2 of the 16 cited wrongful acts implicated petitioning activity).) Therefore, the burden shifted to plaintiffs to show minimal merit to their first claim. The declarations of Adnan Mehmood and his brother meet the second prong. Those declarations show that Ms. Knight breached her fiduciary duty by delaying and withholding payments to the LLC's creditors. The litigation privilege does not preclude liability for this conduct. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Order To Show Cause Why Appointment Of Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed

Parties represent to the Court that they have reached a stipulation regarding this matter. The Court vacates its tentative ruling as to the Order To Show Cause Why Appointment Of Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed, and signs parties' stipulated order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
